title: 1.2 Complexity Analysis
subtitle: Time and space complexity, Big O notation

content.introduction: Complexity analysis helps estimate how algorithm performance scales with input size using time and space measures.

sections.title: Time Complexity
sections.content: Time complexity describes how runtime grows with input size. Common classes include O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), and O(n^2).
examples: Merge sort is O(n log n)

sections.title: Space Complexity
sections.content: Space complexity measures additional memory required. Some algorithms trade extra memory for speed.
examples: In-place sorts use O(1) extra space
Merge sort requires O(n) extra space

sections.title: Big O Notation
sections.content: Big O gives an upper bound on growth rate, focusing on large inputs and ignoring constants.
examples: O(n log n) grows slower than O(n^2)

keyTakeaways: Time and space are the main resources considered
Big O focuses on asymptotic behavior
